assertions.text: Calls out that adding a keyword requires BOTH kwlist.h AND a keyword-classification rule in gram.y (unreserved_keyword / col_name_keyword / etc.)
Notes scan.l does NOT need a change for a new keyword (only for a new token shape)
Cites the transformStmt switch location at analyze.c:334-444 (or :368) and names it specifically
Mentions the load-bearing 'Caution' that transformStmt changes must also update stmt_requires_parse_analysis and analyze_requires_snapshot (analyze.c:363-367 / :468-505 / :512-529)
States T_BlendStmt is auto-generated into nodetags.h and must NOT be hand-edited
Mentions parsenodes.h must already be in gen_node_support.pl's @all_input_files (or cites that list)
Mentions catversion bump is required only if the node ends up in stored catalog trees (rule actions / views)
Mentions the debug GUCs (debug_copy_parse_plan_trees / debug_write_read_parse_plan_trees / debug_raw_expression_coverage_test) for round-trip testing
Mentions walker integration in nodeFuncs.c (expression_tree_walker / mutator / raw walker) if the node holds child expressions
Recommends copyObject(p) as the primary answer
Notes the typeof_unqual macro magic that preserves the argument type without a cast (nodes.h:228-233)
Cites copyfuncs.c with line numbers for copyObjectImpl (e.g. :176-212 or :185)
Explains by-ref Datum handling via _copyConst calling datumCopy with line cite
Calls out check_stack_depth() guard at top of copyObjectImpl (with cite)
Distinguishes T_List (deep, via list_copy_deep) vs T_IntList/T_OidList/T_XidList (shallow list_copy) with copyfuncs.c line cite
Mentions extensible nodes (_copyExtensibleNode) require a registered nodeCopy callback
Notes the copy is allocated in CurrentMemoryContext and switching contexts is on the caller
Connects to the roundtrip copyObject(stringToNode(nodeToString(p))) being load-bearing for plan cache / rules / parallel workers
Recommends expression_tree_mutator (not walker) since the task is rewrite, returning Node *
States the mutator contract: NULL return means 'delete this subtree'
States the walker contract: callback returns true to short-circuit, false to keep going
Explains macro wrapping (nodeFuncs.h:155-183) suppresses callback-type warnings so callbacks can be typed
Distinguishes raw_expression_tree_walker (pre-analysis, ColumnRef/A_Expr) vs expression_tree_walker (post-analysis, Var/OpExpr)
Notes ColumnRef only exists pre-analysis and Var is the post-analysis equivalent — picks the correct walker per stage
Lists ALL of {exprType, exprTypmod, exprCollation, exprLocation, walker, mutator, raw walker} as maintenance points when adding an expression node, with line cites in nodeFuncs.c
Mentions query_tree_walker/mutator flags (QTW_*) from nodeFuncs.h:21-34 for controlling rtable/subquery recursion
Mentions planstate_tree_walker as the executor-time equivalent (rounding out the 3-stage picture)

delta_vs_iter1.with_skill_delta: +0 (already 27/27 in iter-1; edits hardened existing answers and moved content from file-doc into SKILL inline, reducing round-trips)
delta_vs_iter1.qualitative: Iter-1 with_skill passes leaned on the nodeFuncs.c / analyze.c file-docs to enumerate maintenance points and the three-site Caution. Iter-2 SKILL.md now contains those inline with verified line cites, so the same answers are now reachable without the file-doc round-trip. No assertion-level change; quality-of-source improvement.
